{{Infobox event
| name = Valinhall Gate
| introduced = tg-02-crimson-vault
}}

''See also: [[Valinhall Gate Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Valinhall]] Gate serves as a portal connecting the external world to [[the House]] of [[Valinhall]]. These gateways typically manifest when a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]] slices through reality, creating a white-edged swirling doorway that resembles a curtain being drawn back. While these portals can be opened from any location, they possess a unique limitation: any subsequent Gate opened from within [[Valinhall]] will lead the traveler back to the exact location where they first entered. Consequently, the territory is considered inefficient for rapid travel between different destinations. Most [[Gates]] lead directly into [[the House]]'s entry hall, a large room containing furniture, mirrors, and [[sword]] racks. The stability and appearance of a Gate can vary; they may appear ragged, shrink in size, or even manifest as simultaneous slashes of light that push against one another. Specialized tools like the black gauntlet, known as a [[gatecrawler]], can be used to force open recently closed or closing [[Gates]]. Additionally, certain artifacts like the [[frozen horn]] have the power to close these portals.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall]] Gate serves as a one-way portal used for movement, providing a location toward which [[Simon]] can retreat. While these gates are typically only held open by a full [[Traveler]] utilizing an assigned Dragon’s [[Fang]], they can be cut open with a blade. [[Denner]] demonstrated this ability by slicing into a [[gate]], and [[Simon]] later successfully used [[Azura]] to slice downward into one. [[Indirial]] also proved the [[gate]] could be breached by using a blade to slice it open in midair before walking through.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Valinhall Gate]] can be cut open.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Gate]] is a location [[Simon]] can retreat toward.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Valinhall Gate]] can only be held open by a full [[Traveler]] using his assigned Dragon’s [[Fang]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Denner]] can slice open a [[Valinhall Gate]] with his [[sword]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Valinhall Gate]] is a portal used for movement.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] [[Gates]] only go in one direction.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] attempts to cut a [[Valinhall Gate]] using [[Azura]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] sliced downward into a [[Valinhall Gate]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] uses his blade to slice open a Gate in midair and walks through the [[Valinhall Gate]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] can use [[Azura]] to open a [[Valinhall Gate]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] can cut a [[Valinhall Gate]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Valinhall]] Gate can be opened from any location outside by a [[Traveler]] who slices the air from top to bottom, creating a swirling doorway with white edges. While these gates always lead back to [[the House]]'s entry hall, the process of opening one is a lengthy endeavor. Near [[Leah's camp]], residue from an old [[gate]] can still be found, and [[Indirial]] possesses the ability to cut one open. During his travels, [[Simon]] attempts to slice a [[gate]] within his own tent, eventually resulting in an open portal. Ultimately, two [[Valinhall]] [[Gates]] are opened at the same time within the entry hall.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] can cut open a [[Valinhall Gate]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|There is residue of an old [[Valinhall Gate]] near [[Leah's camp]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Opening a [[Valinhall Gate]] takes a long time.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] [[Gates]] can be opened from anywhere outside and always lead to [[the House]]'s entry hall.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13">''City of Light'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A normal [[Valinhall Gate]] is opened by a [[Traveler]] slicing it from top to bottom, creating a white-edged swirling doorway.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] attempts to cut a [[Valinhall Gate]] in his tent.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Valinhall Gate]] can be open.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23">''City of Light'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Two [[Valinhall]] [[Gates]] opened simultaneously in the entry hall.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Events]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate events]]
